                                              , 
                                              w 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              v 
                                              ∧ 
                                              w 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      and  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∪ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           max 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              v 
                                              , 
                                              w 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              v 
                                              ∨ 
                                              w 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                     .  □

It is a well-known result (e.g.,  Davey & Priestley, 1990  Lemma 1.21) that for any partial order  
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      and  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                      we have  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           ≤ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                      if and only if  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ⊆ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                     .

If  
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      is a lattice then for all  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                      the meet  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           ∧ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                      exists, and  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              v 
                                              ∧ 
                                              w 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      is the set of all common lower bounds of  
                                        v 
                                      and  
                                        w 
                                     , which equals  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∩ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                     . So,  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      is closed for intersection, and because there is a top element  
                                        ⊤ 
                                      in  
                                        V 
                                      we have  
                                        
                                           V 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           ⊤ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                     . Conversely, if  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      is closed for intersection and  
                                        
                                           V 
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                     , then for all  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                      there is  
                                        
                                           u 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                      such that  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           u 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∩ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                     . Since  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           u 
                                        
                                      is the set of all common lower bounds of  
                                        v 
                                      and  
                                        w 
                                     , it follows that  
                                        u 
                                      is their greatest lower bound, i.e.,  
                                        
                                           u 
                                           = 
                                           v 
                                           ∧ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                     . This means that meets exist in  
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     , and that  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∩ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              v 
                                              ∧ 
                                              w 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      holds for all  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                     . Moreover,  
                                        
                                           V 
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      implies that there is a top element  
                                        ⊤ 
                                      in  
                                        V 
                                      such that  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           ⊤ 
                                           = 
                                           V 
                                        
                                     . So, for all  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                      the collection of all common upper bounds of  
                                        v 
                                      and  
                                        w 
                                      is not empty. Taking the meet of all these common upper bounds then provides the least upper bound for  
                                        v 
                                      and  
                                        w 
                                     , and thus joins exist.

If  
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      is a linear order then it is a lattice where meets coincide with minima, and joins coincide with maxima. Then  (i)  implies that  
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                           , 
                                           ⊆ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      is a linear order, too. Due to  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∩ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           min 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              v 
                                              , 
                                              w 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      and  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∪ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           max 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              v 
                                              , 
                                              w 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      it follows that  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      is closed for both intersection and union. Conversely, let  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      be closed for intersection and union, and let  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ≤ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                      for  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                     . Then we have to show that  
                                        
                                           w 
                                           ≤ 
                                           v 
                                        
                                     . Suppose  
                                        
                                           w 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ≤ 
                                           v 
                                        
                                     , then  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ⊆ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                      and  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ⊆ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                        
                                     . This implies that  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∪ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                        
                                      is not a principal downset, which contradicts the assumption that  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 O 
                                              
                                              
                                                 π 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              V 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      is closed for union. So, it follows that  
                                        
                                           w 
                                           ≤ 
                                           v 
                                        
                                     , and  
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      is a linear order. For all  
                                        
                                           v 
                                           , 
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                      we then have  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∩ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           min 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              v 
                                              , 
                                              w 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              v 
                                              ∧ 
                                              w 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                      and  
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           v 
                                           ∪ 
                                           ↓ 
                                           w 
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           max 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              v 
                                              , 
                                              w 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           = 
                                           ↓ 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              v 
                                              ∨ 
                                              w 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                     .  □

The proof of  Proposition 1 (iii) makes clear that also in case of  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       forming a lattice, treated in  Proposition 1 (ii), the equation  
                         
                            ↓ 
                            
                               ( 
                               v 
                               ∨ 
                               w 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            ↓ 
                            v 
                            ∪ 
                            ↓ 
                            w 
                         
                       holds whenever  
                         
                            v 
                            ≤ 
                            w 
                         
                       or  
                         
                            w 
                            ≤ 
                            v 
                         
                       for all  
                         
                            v 
                            , 
                            w 
                            ∈ 
                            V 
                         
                      . This can be generalized as stated in  Remark 1  to so-called directed joins, which will be of some importance in the theory developed below.

Definition 3 
                         Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             a partial order, and let  
                               W 
                             be a non-empty subset of  
                               V 
                            . Then  
                               W 
                             is called a  directed set  whenever for all  
                               
                                  v 
                                  , 
                                  w 
                                  ∈ 
                                  W 
                               
                             there exists an element  
                               
                                  u 
                                  ∈ 
                                  W 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ≤ 
                                  u 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  w 
                                  ≤ 
                                  u 
                               
                            .

Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             a partial order, and let  
                               W 
                             be a non-empty subset of  
                               V 
                            . Then  
                               W 
                             is called a  directed set  whenever for all  
                               
                                  v 
                                  , 
                                  w 
                                  ∈ 
                                  W 
                               
                             there exists an element  
                               
                                  u 
                                  ∈ 
                                  W 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ≤ 
                                  u 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  w 
                                  ≤ 
                                  u 
                               
                            .

Remark 1 
                         Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a lattice on the finite set  
                               V 
                            , and let  
                               
                                  W 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  V 
                               
                             be a directed set. Then it is well-known that the join  
                               
                                  ⋁ 
                                  W 
                               
                             exists in  
                               W 
                             (which will be called a  directed join ), and the equation

                                     ( 
                                     ⋁ 
                                     W 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  ⋃ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     ↓ 
                                     w 
                                     : 
                                     w 
                                     ∈ 
                                     W 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             holds.

Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  ,

                                  W 
                               
                             exists in  
                               W

                                     ⋁ 
                                     W 
                                     )

                                  ⋃ 
                                  
                                     {

                                     w 
                                     : 
                                     w 
                                     ∈

                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             holds.

Now, for  
                         
                            
                               p 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                         
                       in  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                       consider

                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     u

                               u 
                               ≤ 
                               v 
                               }

                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       by  
                         
                            (4) 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                                  ↦ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗

                                        K 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           p 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  . 
                               
                            
                         
                      This construction extends the polytomous state  
                         
                            K 
                            : 
                            Q 
                            → 
                            V 
                         
                       to a mapping  
                         
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                            : 
                            Q 
                            → 
                            
                               
                                  O 
                               
                               
                                  π 
                               
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               V 
                               )

                            p 
                            ∈

                                  ↓ 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  .

We will refer to  
                         
                            
                               K 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       as the  extended polytomous (knowledge) state  corresponding to the polytomous state  
                         K 
                      . By definition,  
                         
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                            ∈ 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                         
                       means that ‘at least’ response  
                         v 
                       is given to item  
                         p 
                       with respect to the relation  
                         ≤ 
                      , i.e., the actual response is some  
                         
                            u 
                            ∈ 
                            V 
                         
                       with  
                         
                            v 
                            ≤ 
                            u 
                         
                      .

The extended representation introduced in  (4)  induces a mapping which assigns to each polytomous knowledge structure  
                         K 
                       on  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       the collection  
                         
                            (5) 
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                     : 
                                     K 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      of mappings from  
                         Q 
                       to  
                         
                            
                               
                                  O 
                               
                               
                                  π 
                               
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               V 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      , called an  extended polytomous knowledge structure . Following a convention in knowledge structure theory we introduce the notation  
                         
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                     : 
                                     
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                                  , 
                               
                            
                         
                      which will be useful below.

The following lifts up the results of  Proposition 1  to a characterization of the correspondence between particular collections  
                         K 
                       of elements of  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       and their extended counterparts.  Proposition 2  
                      (ii)  considers response scales forming a lattice and shows that the structure of the downsets characterized in  Proposition 1  
                      (ii)  induces a correspondence between a lattice structure on  
                         
                            ( 
                            K 
                            , 
                            ⊑ 
                            ) 
                         
                       with meets defined item-wise and  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            ⊆ 
                            ) 
                         
                       with  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                            ∈ 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                         
                       being closed for intersection. Assuming a top element in the extended representation is essential for joins to exist. Notice that, in contrast to the subsequently covered situation, the joins need not be defined item-wise.  Proposition 2  
                      (iii)  treats linearly ordered response scales and shows that in this case the downset structure of  Proposition 1  
                      (iii)  induces a correspondence between a lattice structure on  
                         
                            ( 
                            K 
                            , 
                            ⊑ 
                            ) 
                         
                       with joins and meets defined item-wise and  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            ⊆ 
                            ) 
                         
                       being closed for both union and intersection.

Proposition 2 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                               
                             
                            be finite non-empty sets,  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            a partial order, and  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            a non-empty collection of elements of  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                            
                            . 
                         
                         
                            
                               
                                  
                                     (i) 
                                  
                                  
                                     For all  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           L 
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                      
                                     we have  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           ⊑ 
                                           L 
                                           iff 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ⊆ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 L 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           . 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                               
                                  
                                     (ii) 
                                  
                                  
                                     Let  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      
                                     be a lattice with top element  
                                     
                                        
                                           ⊤ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                     
                                     . Then  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           ⊑ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      
                                     is a lattice with top element  
                                     
                                        
                                           Q 
                                           × 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              ⊤ 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                      
                                     and meets defined item-wise if and only if  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                      
                                     is closed for intersection and  
                                     
                                        
                                           Q 
                                           × 
                                           V 
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     . Given these equivalent conditions hold, then  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    ( 
                                                    K 
                                                    ⊓ 
                                                    L 
                                                    ) 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           = 
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∩ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 L 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                      
                                     for all  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           L 
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  
                                     (iii) 
                                  
                                  
                                     Let  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      
                                     be a linear order. Then

                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           ⊑ 
                                           )

                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                      
                                     is closed for union and intersection. Given these equivalent conditions hold, then  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    ( 
                                                    K 
                                                    ⊓ 
                                                    L 
                                                    ) 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           = 
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∩ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 L 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗

                                                    K 
                                                    ⊔ 
                                                    L 
                                                    )

                                           = 
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∪

                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                      
                                     for all  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           L 
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     
                                     .

Let  
                            
                               
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                               
                             
                            be finite non-empty sets,  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            a partial order, and  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            a non-empty collection of elements of  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                            
                            .

(i) 
                                  
                                  
                                     For all  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           L 
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                      
                                     we have  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           ⊑ 
                                           L 
                                           iff 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ⊆ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 L 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           . 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                               
                                  
                                     (ii) 
                                  
                                  
                                     Let  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           V 
                                           , 
                                           ≤ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      
                                     be a lattice with top element  
                                     
                                        
                                           ⊤ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                     
                                     . Then  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           ⊑ 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      
                                     is a lattice with top element  
                                     
                                        
                                           Q 
                                           × 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              ⊤ 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                      
                                     and meets defined item-wise if and only if

                                           K 
                                        
                                      the collection of all common upper bounds of  
                                        K 
                                      and  
                                        L 
                                      is not empty. Taking the meet of all these common upper bounds then provides the least upper bound for  
                                        K 
                                      and  
                                        L 
                                     , and thus joins exist. This construction is reflected by the equation  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    ( 
                                                    K 
                                                    ⊔ 
                                                    L 
                                                    ) 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ∗ 
                                              
                                           
                                           = 
                                           ⋂ 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    M 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ∗ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ∈ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    K 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ∗ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              : 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    K 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ∗ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ∪ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    L 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ∗ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ⊆ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    M 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ∗ 
                                                 
                                              
                                              } 
                                           
                                           . 
                                        
                                      So, we have shown that

                                           K 
                                           , 
                                           ⊑ 
                                           )

                                           Q 
                                           × 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              ⊤ 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           ∈

                                      and meets defined item-wise. 
                               
                               
                                  (iii)

                      (i)  means that  
                         ∗

                            )

Remark 1  can be carried over to  Proposition 2 . In case of  
                         
                            (

                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            )

                            L 
                            ∈ 
                            K 
                         
                       which is comparable for each component  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q

                            K

                               p

                            L

                               p

                            K 
                            
                               ( 
                               p 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      . Then the join  
                         
                            K 
                            ⊔ 
                            L 
                         
                       exists in  
                         K 
                       and is defined item-wise, because  
                         
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               ⊔ 
                               L 
                               ) 
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               p 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            max

                               K

                                  p 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               L

                                  p

                               }

                               ( 
                               p 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      . This may be generalized as above to collections of states.

Definition 4 
                         Let  
                               
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                               
                             be finite non-empty sets, and let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a partial order. A collection  
                               
                                  M 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             is said to be  component-directed  if for each  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                             the subset  
                               
                                  { 
                                  M 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  : 
                                  M 
                                  ∈ 
                                  M 
                                  } 
                               
                             of  
                               V 
                             is a directed set.

Let  
                               
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                               
                             be finite non-empty sets, and let  
                               
                                  (

                                  ,

                                  ) 
                               
                             be a partial order. A collection  
                               
                                  M 
                                  ⊆

                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             is said to be  component-directed  if for each

                             the subset

                                     p
