                                  ∈ 
                                  M 
                                  } 
                               
                             of  
                               V 
                             is a directed set.

Remark 2 
                         Under the conditions of  Proposition 2  
                            (ii) , let  
                               
                                  M 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  K 
                               
                             be component-directed. Then the join  
                               
                                  ⨆ 
                                  M 
                               
                            , which will be called a  component-directed join , is defined item-wise because  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     ⨆ 
                                     M 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  ⋁ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     M 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     : 
                                     M 
                                     ∈ 
                                     M 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  ,

                                  ⋃ 
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           ⨆ 
                                           M 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗

                                           M 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                     :

                                     M 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            .

Under the conditions of  Proposition 2  
                            (ii) , let  
                               
                                  M 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  K

Polytomous knowledge structures with the properties featured in  Proposition 2  will play a central role in the subsequent section.

In complete analogy to the result of  Doignon and Falmagne (1985)  we now provide a characterization of particular types of polytomous knowledge structures in terms of a precedence relation capturing the mutual relationships between the possible responses to all the items. For this we assume that  
                         ≤ 
                       constitutes a partial order on the response values  
                         V 
                      , such that  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       forms a lattice. Given a domain  
                         Q 
                      , we then consider polytomous knowledge structures  
                         K 
                       on  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                      , and binary relations  
                         ≼ 
                       on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      . For  
                         
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  q 
                               
                               
                                  w 
                               
                            
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                       the relation  
                         
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  ≼ 
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        w 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      is to be interpreted as:  
                         If an individual responds at least with  
                               w 
                             to item  
                               q 
                            , then the individual responds at least with  
                               v 
                             to item  
                               p 
                            . 
                       As already mentioned above, ‘at least’ is meant in terms of the partial order  
                         ≤ 
                       on  
                         V 
                      .

Following  Doignon and Falmagne (1985) , a one-to-one correspondence between specific relations on  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                          and particular collections of polytomous knowledge states is established by setting up a Galois connection. Notice that  Proposition 3  does not consider polytomous knowledge structures, but arbitrary collections of subsets of  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         . The characterization of polytomous knowledge structures generalizing the result of  Doignon and Falmagne (1985)  will be obtained in a second step.

                                              ∈

                                        . This in turn is equivalent to

The Galois connection established in  Proposition 3  gives rise to closure operators in the sense of  Definition 5 . In the current application this definition is applied to the partial orders  
                            
                               ( 
                               R 
                               , 
                               ⊆ 
                               ) 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               , 
                               ⊆ 
                               ) 
                            
                         , respectively.

Definition 5 
                            Let  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                be a partial order. A mapping  
                                  
                                     h 
                                     : 
                                     X 
                                     → 
                                     X 
                                  
                                is a  closure operator  on  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               , if for all  
                                  
                                     A 
                                     , 
                                     B 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                                (a)  
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ≤ 
                                     h 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        A 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               ; (b) if  
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ≤ 
                                     B 
                                  
                               , then  
                                  
                                     h 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        A 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     ≤ 
                                     h 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        B 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               ; (c)  
                                  
                                     h 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        h 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           A 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     h 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        A 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               . A subset  
                                  A 
                                of  
                                  X 
                                is called  closed  (with respect to  
                                  h 
                               ) if  
                                  
                                     h 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        A 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     A 
                                  
                               .

Let  
                                  
                                     (

With these notions at hand, we can now draw upon well-known properties of Galois connections (e.g.,  Doignon & Falmagne, 1999  Theorem 6.5), which the subsequent lemma collects.

                                     (i)

Notice that the relation  
                            
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                               
                                  K 
                               
                            
                          in  Proposition 4  may reflect additional structural properties of  
                            ≤ 
                          due to condition  (ii) . The implication in  Proposition 4  
                         (iii)  formalizes an intuitive property. It means that if responding at least  
                            w 
                          to item  
                            q 
                          implies that item  
                            p 
                          is responded to with at least  
                            u 
                          
                         and  with at least  
                            v 
                         , then this conjunction can hold only if the response to item  
                            p 
                          is at least  
                            
                               u 
                               ∨ 
                               v 
                            
                         . Moreover, for linear orders  
                            
                               ( 
                               V 
                               , 
                               ≤ 
                               ) 
                            
                         , where joins coincide with maxima,  Proposition 4  
                         (iii)  holds for any binary relation on  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                         .  Proposition 4  gives rise to the following definition.

Definition 6

                                     V

                                     ) 
                                  
                               ) on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                                satisfying the properties of  Proposition 4  is called a  pre-precedence relation  on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               .

Remark 3 
                            In case of  Proposition 6  
                               (i) , with  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                a lattice, also the component-directed joins in

                                     , 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                are defined item-wise by  Remark 2 .

In case of  Proposition 6  
                               (i) , with  
                                  
                                     (

                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                a lattice, also the component-directed joins in

The following results show that the particular types of collections of subsets of  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                          characterized in  Proposition 6  indeed form the closed elements  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  0 
                               
                            
                          of the Galois connection  
                            
                               ( 
                               f 
                               , 
                               g 
                               ) 
                            
                          established in  Proposition 3 . We start by considering linearly ordered response scales in  Proposition 7 , because in terms of the extended representation its proof is essentially analogous to that of the characterization of quasi-ordinal knowledge spaces in  Doignon and Falmagne (1985) .  Proposition 8  treats response scales with a lattice structure. Its proof is complicated by the fact that the extended representations are not necessarily closed for union, but the existence of unions for the extended counterparts of component-directed collections (see  Remarks 2 and 3 ) is shown to be sufficient for characterizing the closed elements.

Proposition 7 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                
                               be finite non-empty sets, and let  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               be a linear order. Then the collections of subsets of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               that form lattices with joins and meets defined item-wise form the closed elements in  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        0 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               of the Galois connection  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     f 
                                     , 
                                     g 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               established in  
                               
                                  Proposition  3 
                               .

                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               , because  
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                is closed for union. □

Proposition 8 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                
                               be finite non-empty sets, and let  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               be a lattice with top element  
                               
                                  
                                     ⊤ 
                                     ∈ 
                                     V 
                                  
                               
                               . Then the collections of subsets of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               containing  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊤ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               which are lattices with meets defined item-wise form the closed elements in  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        0 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               of the Galois connection  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     f 
                                     , 
                                     g 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               established in  
                               
                                  Proposition  3 
                               .

By  Lemma 1 (iv) the following one-to-one correspondences between the closed elements characterized in  Propositions 5, 7 and 8  are induced.

Corollary 1 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                
                               be finite non-empty sets. If  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               is a linear order then there is a one-to-one correspondence between the pre-precedence relations  
                               
                                  ≼ 
                                
                               for  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               on  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               
                               , and the collections  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               of subsets of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               forming lattices, where all joins and meets are defined item-wise (and coincide with item-wise maxima and minima).

Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                
                               be finite non-empty sets. If  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               is a linear order then there is a one-to-one correspondence between the pre-precedence relations  
                               
                                  ≼ 
                                
                               for  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               on  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               
                               , and the collections  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               of subsets of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               forming lattices, where all joins and meets are defined item-wise (and coincide with item-wise maxima and minima).

Corollary 2 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                
                               be finite non-empty sets. If  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               is a lattice then there is a one-to-one correspondence between the pre-precedence relations for  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               on  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               
                               , and the collections  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               of subsets of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               forming lattices, where all meets (and component-directed joins) are defined item-wise.

Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                
                               be finite non-empty sets. If  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               is a lattice then there is a one-to-one correspondence between the pre-precedence relations for  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               on  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               
                               , and the collections  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               of subsets of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               forming lattices, where all meets (and component-directed joins) are defined item-wise.

The following provides an example for  Corollary 2 .

Example 6 
                            Let  
                                  
                                     V 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        0 
                                        , 
                                        1 
                                        , 
                                        2 
                                        , 
                                        3 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               , and let the binary relation  
                                  ≤ 
                                on  
                                  V 
                                be defined by the line diagram in  Fig. 1 (b). The values in  
                                  V 
                                are interpreted as incorrect (0), partially correct (1, 2), and correct (3) solutions to an item from the domain  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        a 
                                        , 
                                        b 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               . Consider the pre-precedence relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                                that the closure operator  
                                  
                                     g 
                                     ∘ 
                                     f 
                                  
                                of  Proposition 3  associates to the binary relation  
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           
                                              a 
                                           
                                           
                                              3 
                                           
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           
                                              b 
                                           
                                           
                                              3 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                               . The defining feature of  
                                  ≼ 
                                thus is that ‘completely solving problem  
                                  a 
                                is a prerequisite to completely solving problem  
                                  b 
                               ’. The illustration of the pre-precedence relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                by the line diagram in  Fig. 5  highlights this relation through emphasizing the respective line segment. Deriving the corresponding collection of polytomous states then results in  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ≼ 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        
                                           V 
                                        
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∖ 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 a 
                                              
                                              
                                                 0 
                                              
                                           
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 b 
                                              
                                              
                                                 3 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 a 
                                              
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                           
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 b 
                                              
                                              
                                                 3 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 a 
                                              
                                              
                                                 2 
                                              
                                           
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 b 
                                              
                                              
                                                 3 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               , the line diagram of which is illustrated in  Fig. 3 (b). In  Example 3  it was shown that meets are defined item-wise in  
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                               , while joins in general are  not . But, consider, for example the component-directed collection  
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           
                                              a 
                                           
                                           
                                              3 
                                           
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           
                                              b 
                                           
                                           
                                              0 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           
                                              a 
                                           
                                           
                                              2 
                                           
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           
                                              b 
                                           
                                           
                                              1 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                                of polytomous states, the join of which equals  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                        
                                           3 
                                        
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           b 
                                        
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               , and is defined item-wise.

Let  
                                  
                                     V 
                                     =

                                on

                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                               , while joins in general are  not . But, consider, for example the component-directed collection  
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           
                                              a 
                                           
                                           
                                              3 
                                           
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           
                                              b 
                                           
                                           
                                              0 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           
                                              a

                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           b 
                                        
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                               , and is defined item-wise.

As already mentioned, the closed collections of subsets of  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                          characterized in  Corollaries 1 and 2  need not be polytomous knowledge structures on  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         . Confining consideration to these particular closed collections also constrains the binary relations that are in one-to-one correspondence to them. The following definition takes care of this restriction.

Definition 7 
                            Let  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                be finite non-empty sets, and let  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                be a lattice with bottom element  
                                  ⊥ 
                               . Moreover, let  
                                  ≼ 
                                be a binary relation on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               . Then  
                                  ≼ 
                                is called a  precedence relation  for  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V

Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     ,

Proof 
                            
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊤ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ≼ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                holds for any binary relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                on

                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               . 
                            We have  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              Q 
                                              × 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 ⊥ 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              )

                                     = 
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊥ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               . Then for any  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              Q 
                                              × 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 ⊥ 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              )

                                     v 
                                     = 
                                     ⊥ 
                                  
                               , and thus

                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              Q 
                                              ×

                                                 ⊥ 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗

                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊥ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ≼ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . 
                            For each  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           r 
                                        
                                        
                                           u 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                                construct a mapping  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     : 
                                     Q 
                                     → 
                                     V 
                                  
                                by  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              p 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                           = 
                                           ⋁ 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              v 
                                              : 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    p 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    v 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ≼ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    r 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                              
                                              } 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               for all  
                                  
                                     p 
                                     ∈ 
                                     Q 
                                  
                               . From reflexivity of  
                                  ≼ 
                               , and the fact that  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           r 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     ≼ 
                                     
                                        
                                           r 
                                        
                                        
                                           u 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                implies that  
                                  
                                     v 
                                     ≤ 
                                     u 
                                  
                               , it follows that  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        r 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     u 
                                  
                                and thus  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           r 
                                        
                                        
                                           u 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               . It remains to show that  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ≼ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . So, assume  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                        
                                           w 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     ≼ 
                                     
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                        
                                           w 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . Then  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                        
                                           w 
                                        
                                     
                                     ≼ 
                                     
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                        
                                           K 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              q 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     ≼ 
                                     
                                        
                                           r 
                                        
                                        
                                           u 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               , from which

                                           r 
                                        
                                        
                                           u 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                follows by transitivity of  
                                  ≼ 
                               , and thus  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . □

Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊤ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ≼ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                holds for any binary relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               .

We have

                                              Q 
                                              ×

                                                 ⊥

                                     Q 
                                     ×

                                        ⊥ 
                                        }

                                              Q 
                                              ×

                                                 ⊥

                                     = 
                                     ⊥

                                              Q 
                                              ×

                                                 ⊥

                                     Q

                                     V

                                              v

                                     ∈ 
                                     Q

                                  ≼

This leads to the main theorems establishing one-to-one correspondences between precedence relations and particular polytomous knowledge structures with response scales forming linear orders and lattices, respectively.

Theorem 1 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     , 
                                     V 
                                  
                                
                               be finite non-empty sets. If  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               is a linear order then there is a one-to-one correspondence between the precedence relations  
                               
                                  ≼ 
                                
                               for  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               on  
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               
                               , and the polytomous knowledge structures  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               on  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               that form lattices, where all joins and meets are defined item-wise (and coincide with item-wise maxima and minima).

Schrepp (1997)  suggests an extension of KST to a linearly ordered response scale which is assumed to be identical over items. In this approach the precedence relation is conceived as a relation  
                         
                            
                               ≼ 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       on  
                         Q 
                      , where  
                         
                            p 
                            
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                            q 
                         
                       for  
                         
                            p 
                            , 
                            q 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                       is interpreted as ‘every subject reaches a better (or equal) result in solving  
                         p 
                       than in solving  
                         q 
                      ’ ( Schrepp, 1997  p. 238). Within the present framework this interpretation is captured as follows: for any polytomous state  
                         
                            K 
                            ∈ 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         
                       respecting the relation  
                         
                            p 
                            
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                            q 
                         
                       we have  
                         
                            K 
                            
                               ( 
                               q 
                               ) 
                            
                            ≤ 
                            K 
                            
                               ( 
                               p 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      .  Proposition 9  identifies the particular precedence relations for  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                       corresponding to this characterization.

Given a precedence relation  
                         ≼ 
                       for

                       through  
                         
                            (13)

                      for

                            Q 
                         
                      . Then
