                               Q 
                            
                         
                       is a quasi-order on  
                         Q 
                       as considered by  Schrepp (1997) . Recently,  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  provided a characterization of the polytomous knowledge structures which are in one-to-one correspondence to these quasi-orders. Notice that in their approach, as well as in the theory developed here,  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       is not limited to form a linear order as in  Schrepp (1997) , but can be any (complete) lattice. Notice also that  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  allow for infinite sets  
                         Q 
                       and  
                         V 
                      , while the present paper explicitly treats only the finite case.

The approach initiated by  Schrepp (1997)  and fully developed by  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  can be integrated into the present framework. Given a quasi-order  
                         
                            
                               ≼ 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       on  
                         Q 
                      , we may define a binary relation  
                         ≼ 
                       on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                       by  
                         
                            (14) 
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  ≼ 
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        w 
                                     
                                  
                                  iff 
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 v 
                                                 = 
                                                 ⊥ 
                                                 , 
                                                 
                                                 or 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 ⊥ 
                                                 ≠ 
                                                 v 
                                                 ≤ 
                                                 w 
                                                 
                                                 and 
                                                 
                                                 p 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       ≼ 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       Q 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 q 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      for all  
                         
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  q 
                               
                               
                                  w 
                               
                            
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      . It is routine to verify that  (14)  defines a precedence relation for  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      . In fact, the relation defined by  (14)  is the smallest precedence relation that contains the direct product of  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       (i.e., the associated closed element in  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               0 
                            
                         
                      ). So, it is generated by the direct product and differs from it only in that  
                         
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  ⊥ 
                               
                            
                            ≼ 
                            
                               
                                  q 
                               
                               
                                  w 
                               
                            
                         
                       holds for all  
                         
                            p 
                            , 
                            q 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            w 
                            ∈ 
                            V 
                         
                      , independent of the quasi-order  
                         
                            
                               ≼ 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                      . This motivates the subsequent definition, which also introduces a somewhat more general notion, capturing the idea that a prerequisite relation is in line with the response scale.

Definition 8 
                         Let  
                               
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                               
                             be finite non-empty sets, and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             a lattice. Let  
                               ≼ 
                             be a precedence relation for  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             on  
                               
                                  Q 
                                  × 
                                  V 
                               
                             according to  Definition 7 . If  
                               ≼ 
                             satisfies  (14)  for some quasi-order  
                               
                                  
                                     ≼ 
                                  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                             on  
                               Q 
                             then the precedence relation will be called  factorial . Moreover, if  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  ≼ 
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        w 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             implies  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ≤ 
                                  w 
                               
                             for all  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        w 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                                  × 
                                  V 
                               
                             then the precedence relation is said to be  aligned .

Let

Example 7

Notice that applying  (13)  to a factorial precedence relation defined by  (14)  on a given quasi-order  
                         
                            
                               ≼ 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       on  
                         Q 
                       as in

is the identity. Moreover, beyond the obvious fact that any factorial precedence relation is aligned, we have the following results.

Proposition 10 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                               
                             
                            be finite non-empty sets, and  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            a lattice. Let  
                            
                               ≼ 
                             
                            be a precedence relation for  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                            , and let  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            denote the corresponding polytomous knowledge structure on  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                             
                            according to  
                            
                               Theorem  2 
                            . Then the following statements hold. 
                         
                         
                            
                               
                                  
                                     (i) 
                                  
                                  
                                     The precedence relation  
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                      
                                     is aligned if and only if  
                                     
                                        
                                           Q 
                                           × 
                                           
                                              { 
                                              w 
                                              } 
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                      
                                     for all  
                                     
                                        
                                           w 
                                           ∈ 
                                           V 
                                        
                                     
                                     ; 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  
                                     (ii) 
                                  
                                  
                                     If the precedence relation  
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                      
                                     is factorial then all joins in  
                                     
                                        K 
                                      
                                     are defined item-wise.

Let

These results are also part of the characterization of Schrepp’s approach in  Stefanutti et al. (2020) . Notice, however, while in their development they start out by defining joins item-wise, this property here is identified as a consequence of the particular assumptions on the precedence relation on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      . In view of  Proposition 10  
                      (i) , we will also speak of an  aligned  polytomous knowledge structure whenever  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            
                               { 
                               w 
                               } 
                            
                            ∈ 
                            K 
                         
                       for all  
                         
                            w 
                            ∈ 
                            V 
                         
                      .

Subsequently, we will show that the class of aligned polytomous knowledge structures is of particular importance, which emerges from the following construction.

It is therefore interesting to characterize those polytomous knowledge structures for which the restrictions are again polytomous knowledge structures.

Proposition 11

                                  Q 
                                  ,

Proposition 12 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                               
                             
                            be finite non-empty sets, and  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            a lattice with bottom element  
                            
                               ⊥

                                  ,

                                  )

                                                       Y 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       Q 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                      
                                     are isomorphic substructures of  
                                     
                                        K

                                                 q

The polytomous knowledge structures satisfying properties  (i) and (ii)  of  Proposition 12  are said to be  factorial . This proposition corresponds to the main result of  Stefanutti et al. (2020, Theorem 10) , who characterized factorial polytomous knowledge structures  
                         K 
                       in a different way through their atoms and bases that are associated to join-irreducible response values in  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                      . The above development is based on the notion of a restriction of a polytomous knowledge structure instead. While  Proposition 12  
                      (ii)  is merely restating within the present framework what  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  called independent levels, the condition in  Proposition 12  
                      (i)  can be seen as an analogue to their notion of a level-free structure. The characterization by means of isomorphic substructures suggested here draws an even clearer picture of the symmetries inherent in a factorial polytomous knowledge structure.

The theory as developed above shows that all the relevant structural properties are defined item-wise. This not only holds for the definition of the partial order on polytomous states according to  (1) , but also for meets (and joins in case of linearly ordered response values) that exist in the closed polytomous knowledge structures as characterized in  Theorems 1 and 2 . As a consequence we can easily dispense with the assumption that the response scales have to be identical across items. The theory can readily be generalized to allow for response scales that are item-specific, i.e., with response values and respective lattices differing across items.

For each item  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                       let  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                       denote a finite non-empty set of response values, which is partially ordered by  
                         
                            
                               ≤ 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                       so that  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≤ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       is a lattice, with joins and meets denoted by  
                         
                            
                               ∨ 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                       and  
                         
                            
                               ∧ 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                      . In a slight misuse of notation, let  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            
                               
                                  V

                       denote the set

                                  ∈

                               p

                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p

                       value combinations. In contrast to the case of identical response scales, where their characterization as mappings was most natural, polytomous states may best be conceived as particular subsets of  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                         
                       that form transversals in the sense of  Mirsky (1971) .

Having introduced these notions, we can now generalize  Theorem 2  as follows.

Theorem 3 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               Q 
                             
                            be a finite non-empty set, and let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≤ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be a lattice on a finite non-empty set  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                               
                             
                            for each  
                            
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                            . Then there is a one-to-one correspondence between the precedence relations on  
                            
                               
                                  Q 
                                  × 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            , and the polytomous knowledge structures  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            on  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                             
                            that form lattices, where all meets (and component-directed joins) are defined item-wise.

Let  
                            
                               Q

This section employs item tree analysis (ITA;  Schrepp, 1999; van Leeuwe, 1974 ) in a slightly modified form ( Heller, 2019 ). The following generalizes the approach in order to establish a precedence relation on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                         
                       that gives rise to a polytomous knowledge structure on  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                      . Notice that empirical application of the presented theory is by no means dependent upon ITA-based procedures. See  Ünlü et al. (2013)  for an overview on alternative methods to build (dichotomous) knowledge structures from data. ITA was merely chosen to demonstrate that available KST methods immediately generalize to the polytomous case. This is achieved by applying the classical methods developed for the dichotomous case to the extended representation of polytomous knowledge structures.

                         D 
                       denote the multiset of response patterns – conceived as elements of

Being able to infer that at least response

Various indices for measuring the goodness-of-fit of a knowledge structure were suggested in the dichotomous case (for an overview see  Ünlü et al., 2013 ). A widely used class of indices is based on quantifying the discrepancy between the observed data and the assumed knowledge structure through the symmetric set difference between response patterns and knowledge states. This amounts to summing the distance between response and state for each of the items, which is set to  
                         1 
                       if the item is in either but not in both sets, and to  
                         0 
                       otherwise. The following generalizes this idea to the polytomous case. For this we assume that for each  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                       the lattice  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≤ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       is  graded  with rank  
                         n 
                      , which holds if and only if every maximal chain of  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                       has the same length  
                         n 
                      . In a graded lattice  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≤ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       one can introduce a rank function  
                         
                            
                               
                                  ρ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            : 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            → 
                            
                               { 
                               0 
                               , 
                               1 
                               , 
                               … 
                               , 
                               n 
                               } 
                            
                         
                       by defining  
                         
                            
                               
                                  ρ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               v 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                       for all  
                         
                            v 
                            ∈ 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                         
                       as the length of a maximal chain between the bottom element  
                         
                            
                               ⊥ 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                       and  
                         v 
                       (e.g.,  Stanley, 1997 ). The rank function in turn gives rise to a metric  
                         
                            
                               δ

                               p

                                     v

                                     w

                                     v

                                           p 
                                        
                                     
                                     w

                                        p

                                     v

                                           p 
                                        
                                     
                                     w 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      for  
                         
                            v 
                            , 
                            w 
                            ∈ 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                         
                       (e.g.,  Birkhoff, 1948 ). The distance  
                         δ 
                       between a response pattern

                                           p

                                           p 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     )

                             when starting from  
                               1 
                            ), independent of taking the path via  
                               
                                  1 
                                  
                                     
                                        ∨ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  2 
                                  = 
                                  3 
                               
                             or  
                               
                                  1 
                                  
                                     
                                        ∧ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  2 
                                  = 
                                  0 
                               
                            .

For comparing the goodness-of-fit across different polytomous knowledge structures  
                         K 
                      , it can be summarized in a single number for each  
                         K 
                       by averaging the  
                         
                            
                               
                                  δ 
                               
                               
                                  min 
                               
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               R 
                               , 
                               K 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                       over all response patterns in  
                         
                            R 
                            ∈ 
                            D 
                         
                       (for the dichotomous case see  Albert, Schrepp, & Held, 1994; Kambouri, Koppen, Villano, & Falmagne, 1994 ). To account for the size of the respective knowledge structure, this index may be normalized by considering its ratio to the average of  
                         
                            
                               
                                  δ 
                               
                               
                                  min 
                               
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               R 
                               , 
                               K 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                       computed over all response patterns in  
                         
                            R 
                            ∈ 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         
                      , which results in a polytomous analogue of the distance agreement coefficient ( Schrepp, Held, & Albert, 1999 ).

Notice that whenever the  
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≤ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       are linear orders for all of the  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                       then  
                         
                            δ 
                            
                               ( 
                               R 
                               , 
                               K 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                       is identical to the cardinality of the symmetric set difference of the extended representations  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       and  
                         
                            
                               K 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                      . The discrepancy index  
                         
                            
                               δ 
                            
                            
                               min 
                            
                         
                       defined by  (18)  thus again results as a direct generalization of classical KST methodology via the extended representation. Notice also that all of the response scales in the subsequently reported empirical application form graded lattices.

Application of the suggested theory is illustrated for the tiles problems of the 2011 TIMSS study ( Foy et al., 2013 ). They are part of the grade 8 released mathematics items, and consider particular quadratic arrangements of red and black tiles of different sizes.  Fig. 9  introduces the tiles problems.  Table A.6  in the Appendix provides details on the evaluation of the responses according to the TIMSS 2011 user guide for the international database (see  Foy et al., 2013  for details), and the subsequently used response codes. Notice that three of the five items contain response categories that render the associated response value mutually incomparable, so that the respective response scales do not form linear orders.

For the illustrative purpose in the present paper, the data of 8th graders from the English-speaking countries Australia, England, New Zealand, and United States of America are included in the analysis. While a nonresponse (blank) is counted as incorrect, consideration is confined to data sets with no missing values at the considered problems (i.e., without not-reached items). This results in a sample size of  
                         
                            N 
                            = 
                            3845 
                         
                       out of a total of  
                         
                            3860 
                         
                       data sets.

Application of the developed theoretical framework is first demonstrated by considering a response coding that grants partial credit to the solutions of the non-dichotomous problems  
                            a 
                         ,  
                            b 
                         , and  
                            e 
                         . This exemplifies the case of linearly ordered response values as treated in  Theorem 1 . Let the domain be given by  
                            
                               Q 
                               = 
                               
                                  { 
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  , 
                                  e 
                                  }

                               ( 
                               V 
                               , 
                               ≤

                               =

                                  0 
                                  , 
                                  1 
                                  , 
                                  2 
                                  }

                            ≤ 
                          the numeric relation ‘less than or equal’. The response values are interpreted as ‘blank, other incorrect’ (0), ‘partially correct’ (1), and ‘completely correct’ (2). Details on how these categories are formed can be found in  Table A.6  in  Appendix A . The response scale  
                            
                               ( 
                               V 
                               , 
                               ≤ 
                               )

                                     1

                               L

                               0

                               ≤

It is easily checked that  
                            ≼ 
                          defined in this way satisfies all the requirements of  Definition 7 , and thus forms a precedence relation on  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                         . Its illustration in  Fig. 11  highlights the nontrivial relations (i.e., those not following from  
                            ≤ 
                          through  (10) ) by emphasizing the respective line segments. The relation  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     2 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     e 
                                  
                                  
                                     2 
                                  
                               
                            
                          means that if item  
                            e 
                          is completely solved then item  
                            a 
                          is completely solved, too. Moreover, from  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  
                                     1 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  
                                     2 
                                  
                               
                            
                          we can conclude that from a completely correct solution to item  
                            b 
                          we can infer that the solution to item  
                            a 
                          is at least partially correct. With these nontrivial relations the precedence relation  
                            ≼ 
                          is aligned (i.e.,  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                            
                          implies  
                            
                               v 
                               ≤ 
                               w 
                            
                          for all  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               , 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                         ), but not factorial. The relation  
                            
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                          defined according to  (13)  contains the reflexive pairs only, so that the mentioned nontrivial relations violate  (14) .

Fig. 12  illustrates the polytomous knowledge structure  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                          on  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q

                            ≼ 
                          of  Fig. 11  according to  Corollary 1 . The structure consists of  
                            
                               19 
                            
                          polytomous states. It is routine to verify that  
                            
                               ( 
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ≼ 
                                  
                               
                               , 
                               ⊑ 
                               ) 
                            
                          is a lattice with joins and meets given by item-wise maxima and minima. Since  
                            ≼ 
                          is aligned, so is  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                         , meaning that  
                            
                               000 
                               , 
                               111 
                               , 
                               222 
                            
                          are states. The obtained structure provides a close fit to the data, because the response patterns for  
                            
                               3781 
                            
                          of the  
                            
                               3845 
                            
                          students coincide with polytomous states in  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                          (98.3 percent), and all of the states are indeed observed as response patterns.

For evaluating the goodness of fit of  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                         , first, observe that with  
                            
                               P 
                               = 
                               0 
                               . 
                               05 
                            
                          increasing the threshold  
                            L 
                          beyond  
                            
                               56 
                            
                          has no effect on the defined relation, as all corresponding quotients  
                            
                               b 
                               ∕ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  b 
                                  + 
                                  d 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          in the complete data set exceed  
                            P 
                          (see  Table 4  for values up to  
                            
                               L 
                               = 
                               918 
                            
                         ). This means that the polytomous knowledge structure of  Fig. 12  is the smallest one possible with  
                            
                               P 
                               = 
                               0 
                               . 
                               05 
                            
                         . Second, it is obvious that the considered linear order  
                            
                               ( 
                               V 
                               , 
                               ≤ 
                               ) 
                            
                          forms a graded lattice, as there exists one and only one maximal chain (see  Fig. 10 ).

Considering the frequencies of the discrepancies  
                            
                               
                                  δ 
                               
                               
                                  min 
                               
                            
                          between the observed response patterns and the polytomous knowledge structure  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                          illustrated in  Fig. 12  reveals that  
                            
                               3781 
                            
                          response patterns (98.3 percent) coincide with states in  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                         . For another  
                            
                               59 
                            
                          students (1.5 percent) the response value for a single item is at distance  
                            1 
                          to that of the closest state, and for only  
                            5 
                          students there is a discrepancy of  
                            2 
                         . Notice that the latter may result from a distance of  
                            2 
                          on a single item, or from a distance of  
                            1 
                          occurring for two items. Summarizing this information by averaging the minimum discrepancy defined in  (18)  over response patterns results in 0.0179, and normalizing this value as suggested above provides 0.0538 as an analogue of the distance agreement coefficient. An in-depth analysis of the data, which is beyond the scope of the present paper, may try to minimize this indices by selecting thresholds  
                            L 
                          and  
                            P 
                          accordingly, keeping the latter within reasonable bounds.

The theory of knowledge structures (KST) was originally conceptualized for dichotomous items. The framework developed in the present paper generalizes the theoretical conceptions to polytomous items. In particular, it characterizes the polytomous analogue of what has been called a quasi-ordinal knowledge space, which  Doignon and Falmagne (1985)  have shown to be in one-to-one correspondence to a quasi-order on the items, capturing precedence relations between items.  Theorems 2 and 3  provide the respective generalizations of this result. They consider a situation where each item allows for partially ordered response values forming lattices, which were called response scales. In contrast to all previous approaches that try to extend knowledge structures to polytomous items, the present framework is able to handle item-specific response scales that may differ across items.

As the basic technique, which drives the development of the theory, an extended representation of knowledge states is introduced. The set of items is thereby extended to the set of all possible item 
                         × 
                       value combinations. This construction integrates the lattice structure of the response scales into the set-theoretic representation, and lets us conceive the item 
                         × 
                       value combinations as virtual dichotomous items. This in turn allows for directly adopting the line of reasoning from the proofs of dichotomous KST, as in the proof of  Proposition 7 , which is formulated in complete analogy to a proof of the main result for characterizing quasi-ordinal knowledge spaces in dichotomous KST ( Doignon & Falmagne, 1985  Theorem 2.8). Moreover, we may immediately apply classical methods of dichotomous KST, which was demonstrated for building polytomous knowledge structures from data through item tree analysis (ITA).

For item-specific response scales

In the dichotomous case these structures are characterized by the following properties: for each item there exists a unique minimal state containing the item (which is called an  atom ), and each state in the structure can be obtained as the union of some subfamily of atoms (including the empty subfamily) and any such union results in a state. In the developed framework there is a unique atom

                      . Conversely, since all atoms are states, so is the join of any (non-empty) subfamily of atoms. As indicated, there is a subtle difference between these two cases. While in the dichotomous case the empty subfamily need to be included in order to generate the state  
                         0̸ 
                      , in the polytomous case it suffices to consider the non-empty subfamilies only.

Assuming finite sets of items and response values, the previously suggested approach by  Schrepp (1997)  and its characterization by  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  both confine their consideration of quasi-ordinal polytomous knowledge spaces to particular instances of the general structures covered by  Theorem 3 . First of all, the response scales have to be identical across items, and second the precedence relation is defined on the set of items, and not on the set of item 
                         × 
                       value combinations. Integrating this approach into the general framework revealed that it implies that, in case of a precedence relation between two items in Schrepp’s sense, precedence relations have to hold between all item 
                         × 
                       value combinations of these items with identical response values ( Proposition 9 ). This is a very special situation, where both the precedence relation on the item 
                         × 
                       value combinations as well as the corresponding polytomous knowledge structure exhibit a high degree of symmetry. In these knowledge structures isomorphic sublattices of the response scale induce isomorphic substructures ( Proposition 12 ). These kinds of structures were called factorial, a notion which is motivated by the fact that the precedence relation is generated by the direct product of Schrepp’s precedence relation between items and the response scale (see the example in  Fig. 6 ). While the resulting symmetries might be theoretically appealing, they turn out to be too restrictive in the presented empirical application. The TIMSS 2011 data on the tiles problems cannot be represented by a factorial, but only by a general polytomous knowledge structure as introduced in  Definition 2 . Moreover, even the property underlying an aligned precedence relation (i.e.,  
                         
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                            ≼ 
                            
                               
                                  q 
                               
                               
                                  w 
                               
                            
                         
                       implies  
                         
                            v 
                            ≤ 
                            w 
                         
                      ), holding in the present application, is not to be conceived as a condition of rationality that data should meet. Its validity will rather be dependent upon the particular items considered: whenever the skills allowing for partially solving an item  
                         q 
                       also suffice for completely solving item  
                         p 
                      , then the order restriction  
                         
                            v 
                            ≤ 
                            w 
                         
                       is violated.

The presented theory only takes a first step to characterizing polytomous knowledge structures with item-specific response scales. It exclusively treats polytomous structures that are analogous to dichotomous quasi-ordinal knowledge spaces, which actually form lattices. The theorems proved in the manuscript show that the corresponding polytomous knowledge structures are also lattices, whenever all the response scales are assumed to be lattices. Similar characterizations of the polytomous counterparts of knowledge spaces (closed under union, but not necessarily under intersection) and learning spaces (i.e., well-graded knowledge spaces) as particular lattices are likely to be obtained under this assumption. But these cases still have to be worked out. Widening the perspective to arbitrary partially ordered response scales will then lead to far more general polytomous structures, the characterization of which is beyond the scope of the present paper and remains an open problem. Moreover, in order to cover the important class of data obtained in psychological testing based on questionnaires with polytomous (mostly Likert-type) items, it remains to develop a polytomous analogue of the recently provided characterization of (quasi-ordinal) assessment structures ( Heller, 2019 ). The theoretical framework introduced here and, in particular, the method of extending the representation are expected to prove useful for future work in this direction.
